channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump's family circle has different look as he returns to the White House
item.description: When Donald Trump returns to the White House on Monday, his family circle will look a little different than it did when he first arrived eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's family circle has a different look as he returns to the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Donald Trump returns to the White House on Monday, his family circle will look a little different than it did when he first arrived eight years ago. His youngest son, Barron, was in fifth grade back then.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden got an Oval Office letter from Trump and may leave one in the desk himself. It’d be a first
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Ronald Reagan probably didn't realize he was starting a tradition when he wrote a note congratulating his successor and left it in the Oval Office desk drawer after two terms as president. He did that for George H.W. Bush, his successor and vice president of eight years.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Vermont accused in lawsuit of tracking pregnant women considered unsuitable to be mothers
item.description: Vermont’s child welfare agency used baseless allegations to secretly investigate a pregnant woman and win custody of her daughter before the baby's birth, according to a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Who is Sean Curran? Head of Trump's personal detail to be nominated for Secret Service director
item.description: Sean Curran, the head of President-elect Trump's personal security detail, will be tapped to lead the U.S. Secret Service, according to the president's son.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: College students accused of online 'catch a predator' plot appear in court
item.description: Six Assumption University students were in court to face charges in a connection made between a Holyoke woman and a man on Tinder, which resulted in a TikTok plot to confront a falsely accused sexual predator.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Shook, WGN 9

item.title: Navajo Nation firefighters battle wildfires in Los Angeles
item.description: A quick-strike crew of Native American firefighters and journeymen utility workers from the Navajo Nation are helping Los Angeles residents cut through landslides, mangled trees and charred debris of the Eaton Fire that rampaged though the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: John Locher, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: First responders saved a moose that fell through lake ice in New York. Here's how they did it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A bull moose that fell through lake ice in the Adirondack Mountains was saved by New York conservation officials in a laborious cold-water rescue. The moose fell through the ice around 11 a.m.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Children's book author and engineer husband exposed for double life in house of horrors
item.description: After one of their adopted children was hospitalized following harrowing tales of abuse, her parents, a children's author and her husband, were handed lengthy prison sentences.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Carolina man pleads guilty to the carjacking and killing of a New Mexico police officer
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A South Carolina man pleaded guilty Friday to charges that he gunned down a New Mexico state police officer who had stopped to help him, marking a turning point in a case that had been set for trial later this year.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Justices agree to hear Maryland case on parents' rights and LGBTQ books
item.description: The parents, represented by the Becket Fund for Religious Liberty, sued Montgomery County Public Schools in 2023 after the school district said it would no longer honor opt-out requests or notify families if a book referencing gender or sexuality would be read in class.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bribery, fraud charges dropped against former New York Lt. Governor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal prosecutors on Friday dropped bribery and fraud charges against former New York Lt. Gov. Brian Benjamin, citing the death of a cooperating witness against the Democrat who the governor had once chosen to be her second-in-command. Judge J.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida abduction report leads to discovery of U-Haul van filled with mostly Chinese migrants
item.description: A report about an alleged abduction led to the discovery of a group of migrants inside a U-Haul truck in South Florida, police said Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The baby names going 'extinct' in 2025, according to BabyCenter
item.description: Much like the doomed flightless dodo, baby names like “Emory,” “Jamie” or “Remy” are quickly being stamped out by the same humans who once found them so uniquely remarkable.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tunnel used to smuggle migrants, weapons located near El Paso
item.description: A tunnel that has been used for a year is used to smuggle migrants, weapons and cash across the U.S.-Mexico border.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Judges hand down what could be the last punishments for the Jan. 6 riot before pardons from Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal judge who oversaw Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case denounced efforts to rewrite the history of the U.S. Capitol attack Friday as she handed down what she acknowledged may be one of the final punishments for the Jan.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Jersey teacher charged with allegedly sexually assaulting former student, having his child
item.description: A New Jersey teacher is facing charges after police say she is accused of sexually abusing one of her students and having his child.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Cristelli, WGN 9

item.title: Ramaswamy plans run for Ohio governor
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy, the co-chair of President-elect Trump's new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), plans to run for governor of Ohio, two sources confirmed to The Hill on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, Fox 5

item.title: Joan Plowright, Tony Award-winning British actor and widow of Laurence Olivier, dies at 95
item.description: Award-winning British actor Joan Plowright, who with her late husband Laurence Olivier did much to revitalize the U.K.'s theatrical scene in the decades after World War II, has died. She was 95.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump inauguration moved indoors because of freezing temperatures
item.description: President-elect Trump’s inauguration ceremony is being moved indoors because of expected bitter cold Monday in Washington, D.C. Trump and Vice President-elect JD Vance are expected to be sworn in inside the Capitol Rotunda rather than on the West Front of the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Q&A: 'God Bless the USA' singer Lee Greenwood on Donald Trump's inauguration and Bibles
item.description: Forty years ago, Lee Greenwood released “God Bless the U.S.A.,” a patriotic anthem that has become a standard at political gatherings. He's performed the song for a number of presidents, including Ronald Reagan and George H. W. Bush.
item.dc:creator: THE ASSOCIATED PRESS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bruce Willis shakes hands with first responders in rare public appearance
item.description: Amid the devastating wildfires in Southern California, Bruce Willis took time to thank first responders.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: Capital One technical issue delays payments for users: What to know
item.description: Some Capital One users were still waiting to receive deposits and payments on Friday morning after the company experienced a processing disruption.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, WGN 9

item.title: Live updates: Noem pledges to eliminate CBP One app; Senate advances Laken Riley Act
item.description: South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem (R), President-elect Trump's pick to head the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), is appearing for her confirmation hearing before a Senate panel Friday. The purview of DHS involves immigration and federal emergency management — two hot-button topics.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, WGN 9

item.title: FDA will allow Zyn nicotine pouches to stay on the market
item.description: Zyn products will be allowed to stay on the market after federal health officials said Thursday that the popular nicotine pouches can help adult smokers cut back or switch completely.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: O'Reilly: Trump's past mob dealings prepared him to handle Putin
item.description: Bill O'Reilly thinks Trump's willingness to talk with anyone, even the mob, will serve him well when dealing with Putin.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: As Biden warns of an 'oligarchy,' Trump will be flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: President Joe Biden's pointed warning about the U.S. becoming an “ oligarchy ” of tech billionaires will be illustrated at Donald Trump's inauguration, when the world's three richest men will sit on the dais as Trump is sworn in for a second term.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Where are they? Help sought finding those missing after Eaton fire in California
item.description: Anyone with information about the missing individuals was asked to contact the Sheriff's Department's Missing Persons Unit at 323-890-5500.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: Longer prison terms for human smugglers sought by US Justice Department
item.description: "We're trying to get the leaders of these smuggling groups to get longer sentences or those who work with cartels in Mexico to conduct their smuggling.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about RedNote, a TikTok alternative
item.description: TikTok patrons are turning to a new platform in light of the nationwide ban set to shut down the app on Sunday. RedNote, a Chinese short-form video platform, is gaining ground as users look for an alternative to the social media company owned by the China's ByteDance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: David Lynch, director of 'Twin Peaks,' 'Blue Velvet' and more, dies at 78, family says
item.description: David Lynch, the filmmaker and director behind such movies as “Eraserhead,” “Blue Velvet” and the TV series “Twin Peaks,” has died, his family announced in a message posted to his official Facebook page.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Police detain man dressed in fire gear in Pacific Palisades after reported looting
item.description: Editor’s Note: This article has been updated to include new information provided by the L.A. County Sheriff’s Office. LOS ANGELES (KTLA) – Two men, one of whom was dressed as a firefighter, were detained by L.A.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sub-freezing temperatures, sunshine forecast for Trump inauguration
item.description: Sub-freezing temperatures and sunshine are forecast for President-elect Donald Trump’s inauguration Monday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Man sought after 'So I raped you' Facebook message being extradited to US
item.description: French Appeal Court prosecutors said an American man accused of sexually assaulting a Pennsylvania college student in 2013 and later sending her a Facebook message that said, “So I raped you," was being extradited Thursday to the United States.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2025 Grammys to proceed with focus on wildfire relief. Here's what you can expect
item.description: The 2025 Grammy Awards are right around the corner, which means it is time to get those viewing party plans in action. Allow us to help.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: South Carolina man dies of Legionnaires' disease after using bacteria-laden hot tub, lawsuit claims
item.description: Two weeks after using a hot tub in his South Carolina home, 72-year-old Wayn Van Der Plaats was fighting for his life, his body racked by a deadly bacteria that causes Legionnaires' disease.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Israel and Hamas reach a ceasefire deal
item.description: President Joe Biden says Israel and Hamas have agreed to a ceasefire deal to pause the war in Gaza and release hostages.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, KRON4
